preview_text:
The model favors Ecuador heavily, assigning them an 83% win probability. Curaçao enters as a substantial underdog at just 1%, with a 16% draw chance offering the only meaningful alternative to an Ecuador victory.

The spread reflects a lopsided matchup. Ecuador's dominance in the model leaves little room for competitive uncertainty; a draw remains plausible but unlikely, and a Curaçao upset falls outside the model's central scenario. The expected scoreline will almost certainly favor Ecuador.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge noticeably on the underdog. Books and Polymarket both assign Curaçao roughly 6–7% win probability, substantially higher than the model's 1%. The model and market align closely on Ecuador (79–83%) and moderately on draws (13–16%). This gap suggests either the model is more confident in Ecuador's superiority or the market prices in tail-risk scenarios the statistical approach discounts. The dashboard does not adjudicate between them.
